title: Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary
summary: A strict boundary for community volunteers: operate only under authority coordination, in low-risk assigned areas, with check-in, communications, stop rules, and no technical rescue.
content:
A strict boundary for community volunteers: operate only under authority coordination, in low-risk assigned areas, with check-in, communications, stop rules, and no technical rescue.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Report to the official incident lead or police and accept only a defined low-risk task, area, time window, reporting channel, and return point.
Work in pairs or larger teams, record departure and return, carry identification, and keep enough energy, water, light, and navigation capacity to withdraw.
Observe and report from safe ground; mark locations without moving evidence, entering structures, approaching cliffs, water, fire, utilities, hazardous materials, or aggressive animals.
Stop and return immediately for lost communication, worsening weather, darkness beyond plan, injury, fatigue, uncertain position, or any hazard outside the assignment.
warnings: Volunteers must never self-deploy into an active collapse, flood, wildfire, or police search area.
Finding a person does not authorize moving them unless immediate danger requires it and emergency dispatch directs you.
common_mistakes: Starting Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Untrained volunteer search-team safety boundary.']
